howItWorks.intro: This wood glue calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Area = Joint Length × Joint Width × Number of Joints × 2 (both surfaces)
Glue Needed (oz) = Total Area ÷ Coverage Rate
PVA coverage ≈ 25 sq in/oz, Polyurethane ≈ 20 sq in/oz
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How much wood glue do I need?
faq.answer: Apply a thin, even coat covering the entire joint surface. PVA glue covers about 25 square inches per ounce. A standard 8oz bottle handles most small to medium projects.

faq.question: Should I apply glue to both surfaces?
faq.answer: For PVA (yellow) glue, applying to both surfaces gives the strongest bond. For polyurethane glue, apply to one surface and dampen the other with water.

faq.question: How long should I clamp wood glue?
faq.answer: PVA: 30-60 minutes minimum, 24 hours for full cure. Polyurethane: 1-2 hours clamp, 24 hours full cure. Epoxy: varies by formula, 4-24 hours.
